compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can rely on fix-bug to run the scope-drift reviewer after fix verification and before commit
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Integrate the scope-drift-reviewer agent into the fix-bug workflow as a new step between Step 7 (verify fix) and Gates 2a-2d. Add the scope_drift gate signal to the escalation router, add scope_drift.enabled config key, and enforce hard-fail when the agent file is missing.
**Why**: The scope-drift reviewer agent exists but is not wired into the fix-bug pipeline. This story makes it an active gate that blocks behavioral drift before commit.
**Scope**:
- IN: Modify plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md to add new step between Step 7 and Gates 2a-2d, extend gate signal accumulator to include scope_drift signal, extend escalation router primary-signal counting, add scope_drift.enabled config key to .claude/dso-config.conf (default: true), enforce hard-fail when scope-drift-reviewer.md is missing (Read tool error must abort, not silently skip), handle major-drift → user prompt, minor-drift → warning
- OUT: Agent definition itself (Story 1), intent-search changes (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-bug runs the scope-drift reviewer after Step 7 verification and before Gates 2a-2d, passing the ticket text, root_cause_report, git diff, and investigation-identified files to the agent
  ← Satisfies: "Both enhancements integrate into the existing fix-bug workflow"
- When this story is complete, major-drift classification from the reviewer blocks the commit and prompts the user for explicit approval; minor-drift emits a warning and proceeds; no-drift proceeds without friction
  ← Satisfies: "Fixes that introduce behavioral changes not described in the ticket are caught by the scope-drift reviewer after implementation and before commit"
- When this story is complete, the scope_drift gate signal is included in the escalation router primary-signal count and existing Gate 1a/1b/2a-2d signal contracts remain unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "The gate escalation router primary-signal counting logic is extended to include the new signal"
- When this story is complete, fix-bug hard-fails with a clear error when the scope-drift-reviewer.md agent file is missing, rather than silently skipping the review
  ← Satisfies: Scenario: "fix-bug must hard-fail when scope-drift-reviewer agent definition is missing"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Gate signal must follow gate-signal-schema.md contract — scope_drift uses signal_type: primary
- [Reliability] Hard-fail enforcement: Read tool error on missing agent file must abort the step, not fall through to Gates 2a-2d
- [Reliability] Config key scope_drift.enabled must be read via read-config.sh; when false, skip the scope-drift step entirely

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): SCOPE_DRIFT_OUTPUT must be added to the hardcoded printf list in the Escalation Routing section (lines 917-922) that feeds gate-escalation-router.py. Without this, the scope-drift gate signal never reaches the router.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): Non-interactive mode handling required. Major-drift in non-interactive mode (FIX_BUG_INTERACTIVE=false) must defer as INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED ticket comment and proceed, consistent with existing dialog-path deferral pattern. Minor-drift warning emits regardless of interactivity mode.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): Scope-drift step must specify exact position. Assign as Step 7.1 (after Step 7 verify fix, before Gates 2a-2d). Step 7.5 anti-pattern scan precondition must be updated to include scope-drift passage.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Reliability] Scope-drift reviewer dispatch needs sub-agent context detection. When fix-bug runs as sub-agent inside debug-everything, Agent tool is unavailable. Use inline-fallback pattern (read agent .md file and execute instructions directly) consistent with existing intent-search dispatch pattern.
comments.author: Test
